title: genepicoloc
text:
genepicoloc provides a scalable pipeline for genetic colocalization analysis across molecular and clinical trait datasets, with a focus on kidney disease genetics.



Standalone coloc - built-in ABF colocalization, no external dependencies
Preprocessing - liftOver, variant naming, p-value underflow handling
Scalable pipeline - parallel processing for millions of tests
Public data integration - eQTL Catalogue, GWAS Catalog

title: genepicoloc 0.9.3
text:
Restructuring of pipeline architecture for improved scalability

Introduced three-level hierarchy: genepicoloc_wrapper() → genepicoloc_job() → genepicoloc_run()
Implemented two-level parallelization strategy (regions × datasets)
Added job-based processing with configurable max_regions_per_job parameter
Improved memory efficiency: each job needs <1Gb when setting <100 regions (flexible)






Added tar archive storage for efficient file management

Summary statistics saved to sumstats.tar
Colocalization results saved to coloc.tar

Added save_sumstats parameter (default: FALSE) to save processed summary statistcs
Added p_min_save parameter for saving only significant summary statistics
Added p_filt parameter for additional p-value filtering
Added batch_size parameter for controlling Level 2 parallelization
New filter_significant_regions() function to reduce storage requirements
New consolidate_coloc_results() function for study-wise result aggregation
Enhanced gene annotation with configurable nearest parameter
Updated documentation for pipeline functions
Removed dependency on writexl package for core functionality

title: Kidney Genomics Colocalization Atlas
text:
Overview
The kidney atlas integrates colocalization results from CKDGen round 4 eGFR GWAS (Wuttke et al. 2019) against:

Proteomics: UKB PPP (2,940 proteins), Icelanders pGWAS (4,907 proteins)
Metabolomics: GCKD plasma (1,297) and urine (1,409) metabolites
Transcriptomics: Kidney eQTL, eQTLGen (blood)
PheWAS: MVP EUR (1,669 traits), FinnGen (2,272 endpoints), UKB TOPMed (1,419 phenotypes)
Imaging: UKB kidney MRI volumes (4 phenotypes)
Kidney traits: CKDGen r4 (eGFR, BUN, UACR, urate, gout, MA)

title: Getting Started with genepicoloc
text:
genepicoloc provides a scalable pipeline for genetic colocalization analysis across molecular and clinical trait datasets. It includes a standalone implementation of the approximate Bayes factor (ABF) colocalization method, preprocessing tools (liftOver, variant naming), and integration with public resources (eQTL Catalogue, GWAS Catalog).
The typical workflow:

Preprocess your GWAS summary statistics (liftOver, variant naming)
Identify significant genomic regions
Define target datasets (eQTL, pQTL, PheWAS)
Run colocalization across all region-dataset combinations
Annotate and explore results



Throughout this vignette, we use the CKDGen round 4 eGFR GWAS (Wuttke et al. 2019, PMID: 31152163) as a running example. This analysis identified 259 genome-wide significant loci and tested colocalization against 11 molecular and clinical datasets (~16,000 phenotypes), producing 6,439 high-confidence colocalizations.

title: Getting Started with genepicoloc
text:
Installation

# Install from GitHub
remotes::install_github("genepi-freiburg/genepicoloc")

System requirements: bgzip, tabix (from htslib), and tar must be available on your PATH.
For preprocessing (optional): UCSC liftOver binary and chain file.

title: Getting Started with genepicoloc
text:
Step 1: Preprocess Summary Statistics

If your GWAS is already in GRCh38
Load and format directly:

library(genepicoloc)
library(data.table)

sumstats_1 <- fread("my_gwas_hg38.txt.gz")

# Rename to standard columns
sumstats_1 <- match_cols(
  sumstats = sumstats_1,
  Name = "variant_id",    # chr:pos:ref:alt format
  rsID = "rsid",          # optional, can be NA
  CHR  = "chromosome",
  POS  = "position",
  A1   = "effect_allele",
  A2   = "other_allele",
  BETA = "beta",
  SE   = "se",
  nlog10P = "nlog10p",    # -log10(p-value)
  AF   = "eaf",           # needed for quantitative traits
  N    = "n"              # needed for quantitative traits
)



If your GWAS is in hg19
Most published GWAS use GRCh37/hg19 coordinates. Use genepi_liftover() to convert:

# CKDGen eGFR example (hg19 -> hg38)
sumstats_raw <- fread("20171016_MW_eGFR_overall_ALL_nstud61.dbgap.txt.gz")

sumstats_hg38 <- genepi_liftover(
  sumstats = sumstats_raw,
  CHR_name = "Chr",
  POS_name = "Pos_b37",
  A1_name  = "Allele1",
  A2_name  = "Allele2",
  liftOver_bin   = "liftOver",
  liftOver_chain = "hg19ToHg38.over.chain.gz"
)

# IMPORTANT: genepi_liftover() returns a data.frame
# Convert back if you need data.table syntax
setDT(sumstats_hg38)

The function adds CHR_hg38, POS_hg38, A1_hg38, A2_hg38 columns.
Where to get liftOver:

Binary: https://hgdownload.soe.ucsc.edu/admin/exe/linux.x86_64/liftOver
Chain: https://hgdownload.soe.ucsc.edu/goldenPath/hg19/liftOver/hg19ToHg38.over.chain.gz



Add variant names and rsIDs
To standardize variant naming (chr:pos:ref:alt) and add rsIDs:

sumstats_named <- name_by_position(
  sumstats = sumstats_hg38,
  CHR_name = "CHR_hg38",
  POS_name = "POS_hg38",
  A1_name  = "A1_hg38",
  A2_name  = "A2_hg38",
  dbSNP_dir = "/path/to/ensembl_vcf/"
)

Per-chromosome Ensembl VCF files can be downloaded from: https://ftp.ensembl.org/pub/release-115/variation/vcf/homo_sapiens/


Calculate -log10(P)

# Standard case
sumstats_hg38$nlog10P <- -log10(sumstats_hg38[["P-value"]])

# For extreme p-values (e.g., p < 1e-324)
sumstats_hg38$nlog10P <- handle_underflow(
  sumstats_hg38[["P-value"]],
  return_nlog10P = TRUE
)



Required columns



Column
Description
Required




Name
chr:pos:ref:alt (e.g., chr16:20350000:A:G)
yes

optional (can be NA)

title: Getting Started with genepicoloc
text:
Step 2: Identify Significant Regions

coloc_regions_list <- get_coloc_regions(
  sumstats = sumstats_1,
  CHR_name = "CHR",
  POS_name = "POS",
  nlog10p_value_name = "nlog10P",
  nlogP_threshold = 7.30103,   # p = 5e-8
  halfwindow = 500000           # 500 kb each side
)

# CKDGen eGFR: 259 significant regions across 22 chromosomes
nrow(coloc_regions_list$coloc_regions_PASS)

Write regions to indexed files for fast retrieval:

sumstats_1_file <- write_regions(
  coloc_regions_list,
  sumstats_name = "output/eGFR"
)

title: Getting Started with genepicoloc
text:
Step 3: Define Target Datasets

Option A: YAML configuration (recommended for custom analyses)
# studies.yaml
eQTLGen:
  description: "eQTLGen blood eQTL"
  query_function: retrieve_sumstats_tabix
  type: quant
  sdY: 1
  files:
    - /path/to/eQTLGen/cis_eQTLs_hg38.tsv.gz

UKB_PPP:
  description: "UK Biobank plasma proteomics"
  query_function: retrieve_sumstats_tabix
  type: quant
  sdY: 1
  path: /path/to/UKB_PPP/
  pattern: "*.txt.gz"

args_df <- create_args_df_from_config("studies.yaml")



Option B: eQTL Catalogue API

# Browse available datasets
datasets <- get_datasets_eQTL_Catalogue()

# Create args for GTEx kidney cortex
eqtl_args <- make_eQTL_Catalogue_args(
  QTS = "QTS000015",
  quant_method = "ge"
)

title: Getting Started with genepicoloc
text:
Step 4: Run Colocalization

sumstats_1_args <- list(
  coloc_regions_PASS = coloc_regions_list$coloc_regions_PASS,
  sumstats_1_function = "retrieve_sumstats_tabix",
  sumstats_1_file = sumstats_1_file,
  sumstats_1_type = "quant",   # or "cc" for case-control
  sumstats_1_sdY = NA
)

genepicoloc_wrapper(
  dir_out = "output/eGFR_coloc",
  sumstats_1_args = sumstats_1_args,
  args_df = args_df,
  mc_cores = 10
)

The CKDGen eGFR atlas ran 259 regions x 15,930 datasets = 4.1 million colocalization tests in ~17 hours on 20 cores.

Saving regional summary statistics
By default, only colocalization results (posterior probabilities) are saved. To also save the underlying summary statistics for each region-dataset pair (needed for regional association plots), use save_sumstats = TRUE:

genepicoloc_wrapper(
  ...,
  save_sumstats = TRUE,
  force_save_sumstats = TRUE  # skip interactive confirmation
)

Warning: This generates substantial disk usage (tens of GB for large runs). Use only when you need regional plots or downstream fine-mapping.


Standalone colocalization
For simple pairwise tests without the full pipeline:

# Prepare datasets
d1 <- list(
  beta = sumstats_1$BETA,
  varbeta = sumstats_1$SE^2,
  snp = sumstats_1$Name,
  type = "quant",
  sdY = 1
)

d2 <- list(
  beta = sumstats_2$BETA,
  varbeta = sumstats_2$SE^2,
  snp = sumstats_2$Name,
  type = "quant",
  sdY = 1
)

# Run colocalization (no external dependencies)
result <- coloc_abf(d1, d2)

# Posterior probabilities
result$summary
#>     nsnps PP.H0.abf PP.H1.abf PP.H2.abf PP.H3.abf PP.H4.abf
#>      1500     0.000     0.000     0.000     0.015     0.985

title: Getting Started with genepicoloc
text:
Step 5: Annotate Results
After the pipeline completes:

# Results are automatically consolidated into per-study files
# in output/eGFR_coloc/unfilt/

# Load combined filtered results
results <- readRDS("output/eGFR_coloc/annot/annot_filt.RDS")

title: Getting Started with genepicoloc
text:
Key Concepts

Hypotheses
Colocalization tests 5 hypotheses:

H0: No association with either trait
H1: Association with trait 1 only
H2: Association with trait 2 only
H3: Both associated, different causal variants
H4: Both associated, shared causal variant (colocalization)

PP.H4 > 0.8 is the standard threshold for evidence of colocalization.


sumstats_1 vs sumstats_2

sumstats_1 (primary): your GWAS of interest - the “query”
sumstats_2 (secondary): datasets to colocalize against - the “targets”

title: GWAS Colocalization Project - Technical Direction
text:
Public-facing showcase for an R package for GWAS colocalization analysis, deployed on personal VPS infrastructure.



yourname.de        → Astro static site (landing page, docs, project info)
app.yourname.de    → Shiny app (interactive colocalization analysis)
Both served via Nginx, SSL via Let’s Encrypt, admin access via WireGuard VPN.



Considered adding TypeScript/Astro directly into Shiny but decided against it. Shiny is a data exploration tool, not a website builder - mixing frameworks creates unnecessary complexity. Clean separation is the right approach.






Static site generation - fast, minimal JS sent to browser
TypeScript for any interactive elements
Project description, method explanation, links to Shiny app and R package
Impressum + Datenschutz (use personal work/institute address)





Core colocalization analysis and results
Interactive plots via LocusZoom.js for regional association plots
Scrollable genome-wide Manhattan via GenomeSpy
Both embedded via htmlwidgets or direct JS integration
AI interpretation of coloc results via Claude API (server-side, key never exposed to frontend)





Shiny passes coloc statistics to Claude API
Claude returns plain-language interpretation of results
e.g. “Strong evidence of shared causal variant between trait A and B at locus X…”
Goal: publication-style results paragraph output
Consider letting users supply their own API key for public deployment





TypeScript basics (1-2 weeks)
  → Astro (build the landing page as first real project)
    → JS visualization libraries (LocusZoom.js / GenomeSpy)
      → Docker cleanup of full deployment




Personal domain + multiple VPS machines (already running)
WireGuard VPN connecting VPS + home GPU server
Home GPU server available for heavy compute if needed
Avoid university hospital IT (too restrictive for public-facing projects)







Library
Purpose




LocusZoom.js
Regional association plots, colocalization display


GenomeSpy
Scrollable genome-wide Manhattan, GPU accelerated


Astro
Static site / landing page


Claude API
AI interpretation of coloc results

title: CLAUDE.md - genepicoloc Package Development
text:
genepicoloc is an R package for genetic colocalization analysis across multiple QTL and PheWAS datasets. It wraps the coloc package with scalable infrastructure for large-scale analyses.
Repository: https://github.com/genepi-freiburg/genepicoloc



The package powers a large-scale colocalization analysis for the CKDGen Consortium (kidney function GWAS). Key achievements:



7 CKDGen traits: eGFRcr, eGFRcys, UACR, Calcium, Phosphate, Albumin, Urate
9 external data sources: GTEx eQTL, eQTLGen, UKB_PPP (proteomics), Icelanders pGWAS, FinnGen, MVP, UKB_TOPMed, GCKD metabolomics, CKDGen internal
~200,000+ colocalization tests per study
Results stored efficiently in tar archives with job-based parallelization




A production-ready Shiny app was developed: ckdgen-coloc-app/
Features: - Network visualization (region-centered and trait-centered views) - Interactive filtering by PP.H4 threshold and study - Regional Association Plots (LocusZoom-style) with interactive plotly - Gene Info Panel with annotations from NCBI, Reactome, HPO, HGNC - AI-powered insights (Claude API integration) - Docker deployment ready
Key files to reference: - /home/oleg/Work/bioinfo/ckdgen/git/followup/genepicoloc/ckdgen-coloc-app/ - Full Shiny app - /home/oleg/Work/bioinfo/ckdgen/git/followup/genepicoloc/ckdgen_genepicoloc_functions.R - CKDGen-specific helper functions



Successfully implemented regional plot data extraction:
Workflow: 1. Run genepicoloc with save_sumstats=TRUE to save sumstats_2 RDS files in tar archives 2. Extract regional data using optimized job-grouped extraction (5-10x faster) 3. Serve in Shiny app with interactive LocusZoom-style plots
Key optimizations discovered: - Group regions by job, extract tar once per job (not per region) - Deduplicate trait names before processing - Use filename for Icelanders_pGWAS (short name annotation has errors)




Second use case: colocalization for GCKD multi-biomarker GWAS (VAE-derived modules).



ME19 and ME30 modules, SNPTEST format (.gwas), hg19, N=4,850
Severe p-value underflow: lead SNPs have p ~ 1e-1762 (z ~ 90)
pvalue_precise_normal column preserves full precision as text





Load .gwas file with fread()
Handle underflow: handle_underflow(pvalue_precise_normal, return_nlog10P = TRUE)
LiftOver hg19 -> hg38: genepi_liftover() + setDT() on result
Column mapping: SNP->rsID, coded_all->A1, noncoded_all->A2, AF_coded_all->AF, n_total->N
Build Name: chr:pos:ref:alt format





ME19: 1 region (chr2/NAT8), 7 high-confidence colocalizations (creatinine, eGFR, Mg, CKD, BUN, AST)
ME30: 2 regions (chr10/HPSE2 lead signal nlog10P=3274.8, chr2/NAT8 secondary), 12 high-confidence colocalizations
All colocalizations at chr2/NAT8 locus - kidney function signal
chr10/HPSE2 region: no MVP colocalizations (may colocalize with eQTL/pQTL)





Analysis script: genepicoloc_internal/analyses/GCKD_VAE/run_GCKD_VAE_coloc.R
Output: /g/epi/data/studies/00_GCKD/01_analyses/gwas/multi_biomarker_GWAS/common_chip/21_VAE/02_output/genepicoloc/
Summary: ~/Work/bioinfo/genepicoloc/VAE/genepicoloc/email.md





R/
├── colocalization.R      # Core coloc wrapper, map_over_args(), genepicoloc_wrapper()
├── config.R              # YAML-based study configuration system
├── preprocessing.R       # Sumstats formatting, match_cols()
├── get_coloc_regions.R   # Identify significant regions
├── gene_annotation.R     # Gene annotation utilities
├── query_sumstats_custom.R # Data retrieval functions (incl. UKB kidney volumes)
├── ebi_interface.R       # eQTL Catalogue API
├── name_by_position.R    # Variant naming utilities
├── genepicoloc_susie.R   # SuSiE/coloc.susie integration (experimental)
├── shiny.R               # Built-in Shiny viewer
├── utils.R               # Utility functions
└── zzz.R                 # Package loading






match_cols() - Format input sumstats
get_coloc_regions() - Identify significant genomic regions
write_regions() - Save and index preprocessed data
create_args_df() - Create arguments for colocalization
map_over_args() / genepicoloc_wrapper() - Run colocalization
coloc_out_annotate() - Annotate results with gene info





make_eQTL_Catalogue_args() - eQTL Catalogue datasets

Converts genomic coordinates from hg19 to hg38 using UCSC liftOver.
Location: R/name_by_position.R
Usage:
sumstats_hg38 <- genepi_liftover(
  sumstats = sumstats,
  CHR_name = "Chr",
  POS_name = "Pos_b37",
  A1_name = "Allele1",
  A2_name = "Allele2",
  liftOver_bin = "/path/to/liftOver",
  liftOver_chain = "/path/to/hg19ToHg38.over.chain.gz"
)
Output columns: CHR_hg38, POS_hg38, A1_hg38, A2_hg38
Features: - Works on a copy (doesn’t modify input) - Proper temp file handling with on.exit() cleanup - Auto-detects chr prefix format - Handles strand flipping during liftOver
Required tools: - UCSC liftOver binary: https://hgdownload.soe.ucsc.edu/admin/exe/linux.x86_64/liftOver - Chain file: https://hgdownload.soe.ucsc.edu/goldenPath/hg19/liftOver/hg19ToHg38.over.chain.gz



Adds rsID and standardized variant names by querying Ensembl VCF files.
Location: R/name_by_position.R
Usage:
# Option 1: Single VCF file (e.g., chr21 only for testing)
sumstats_named <- name_by_position(
  sumstats = sumstats_hg38,
  CHR_name = "CHR_hg38",
  POS_name = "POS_hg38",
  A1_name = "A1_hg38",
  A2_name = "A2_hg38",
  dbSNP_file = "/path/to/homo_sapiens-chr21.vcf.gz"
)

# Option 2: Per-chromosome VCF directory (recommended for production)
sumstats_named <- name_by_position(
  sumstats = sumstats_hg38,
  CHR_name = "CHR_hg38",
  POS_name = "POS_hg38",
  A1_name = "A1_hg38",
  A2_name = "A2_hg38",
  dbSNP_dir = "/path/to/ensembl_vcf/"  # contains homo_sapiens-chr{1..22,X,Y,MT}.vcf.gz
)
Parameters: - dbSNP_file: Path to single tabix-indexed VCF file - dbSNP_dir: Path to directory with per-chromosome files (homo_sapiens-chr{CHR}.vcf.gz)
Output columns: rs (rsID), Name_hg38 (chr:pos:ref:alt format)
Features: - Works directly with Ensembl VCF files (no preprocessing needed) - Supports both single-file and per-chromosome directory modes - Auto-detects chromosome naming convention (chr21 vs 21) per file - Handles multi-allelic variants (splits comma-separated ALT alleles) - Matches alleles accounting for strand flipping - Skips chromosomes without corresponding VCF file (warns user) - No external dependencies (uses system2 + tabix)
Ensembl VCF source:
# For testing (chr21 only, ~165MB):
wget https://ftp.ensembl.org/pub/release-115/variation/vcf/homo_sapiens/homo_sapiens-chr21.vcf.gz
wget https://ftp.ensembl.org/pub/release-115/variation/vcf/homo_sapiens/homo_sapiens-chr21.vcf.gz.csi

# For production (all chromosomes, ~15GB total):
for chr in {1..22} X Y MT; do
  wget https://ftp.ensembl.org/pub/release-115/variation/vcf/homo_sapiens/homo_sapiens-chr${chr}.vcf.gz
  wget https://ftp.ensembl.org/pub/release-115/variation/vcf/homo_sapiens/homo_sapiens-chr${chr}.vcf.gz.csi
done
Performance (chr21): - Input: 112,722 variants - Matched: 112,722 variants (100%) - Processing time: ~2 minutes



library(data.table)
library(genepicoloc)

# 1. Load hg19 summary statistics
sumstats <- fread("gwas_hg19.txt.gz")

# 2. LiftOver to hg38
sumstats_hg38 <- genepi_liftover(
  sumstats = sumstats,
  CHR_name = "Chr",
  POS_name = "Pos_b37",
  A1_name = "Allele1",
  A2_name = "Allele2",
  liftOver_bin = "liftOver",
  liftOver_chain = "hg19ToHg38.over.chain.gz"
)

# 3. Add rsID and Name (optional, for display)
sumstats_named <- name_by_position(
  sumstats = sumstats_hg38,
  CHR_name = "CHR_hg38",
  POS_name = "POS_hg38",
  A1_name = "A1_hg38",
  A2_name = "A2_hg38",
  dbSNP_file = "homo_sapiens-chr21.vcf.gz"
)

# 4. Calculate -log10(P) with underflow handling
sumstats_named[, nlog10P := -log10(P)]
sumstats_named[!is.finite(nlog10P), nlog10P := max(nlog10P[is.finite(nlog10P)]) + 1]






Results are stored in tar archives per job: - jobs/job_XXX/coloc_results.tar - Colocalization results - jobs/job_XXX/sumstats.tar - Sumstats for regional plots (if save_sumstats=TRUE) - jobs/job_XXX/sumstats_1.RDS - Source study sumstats



After colocalization, coloc_out_annotate() in genepicoloc_internal: 1. Reads consolidated results from dir_out/unfilt/ 2. Dispatches to study-specific annotator: - annotate_eQTL() - GTEx, eQTLGen, Kidney_eQTL (uses gencode_v26 for gene mapping) - annotate_pQTL() - UKB_PPP, Icelanders, GCKD_pGWAS (uses olink protein map) - annotation_std() - GWAS/PheWAS studies (uses annotation DB for phenotype names) 3. Writes annot/study_annot_unfilt.RDS 4. Filters in 3 tiers: coloc_done -> PP.H4 > 0.8 -> sumstats_2 genome-wide sig 5. Final: annot/annot_filt.RDS (also .xlsx)



genepi_liftover() returns data.frame - Returns base R data.frame, not data.table - Must call setDT() on result before using data.table syntax (:=, bare column names in [)
MVP annotation DB has old /data/ paths - annotation_db.RDS was created with old /data/ mount paths - Fix in arguments_custom.R: sub("^/data/", "/g/epi/data/", ...) on file paths from DB - Proper fix: regenerate annotation DB with create_annotation_db()
MVP ancestry-split annotation - MVP_R4_AFR/AMR/EAS/EUR/META studies share the MVP_R4 annotation DB entry - annotation_std() maps grepl("^MVP_R4_", study) to “MVP_R4” for DB lookup
P-value underflow handling - Some GWAS have p-values far beyond R’s numeric range (e.g., 1e-1762 for GCKD VAE) - handle_underflow() parses scientific notation text strings directly - Fallback from z-scores: nlog10P = -log10(2) - pnorm(abs(z), lower.tail=FALSE, log.p=TRUE) / log(10) - Always check for text-format p-value columns (e.g., pvalue_precise_normal in SNPTEST output)
MVP variant naming bug (historical) - Old MVP data had mismatched variant names - Solution: Always verify nsnps is reasonable (~6000, not ~20)
Non-positive definite LD matrices (coloc.susie) - Reference panel mismatch causes negative eigenvalues - Solution: Eigenvalue shift method in shift_eigenvalues_minimal()
Icelanders_pGWAS protein naming - Short name annotation has errors (wrong proteins labeled) - Solution: Use filename (contains SeqId + protein name)
UKB_PPP protein complex duplicates - Multi-protein assays (e.g., FUT3_FUT5) create duplicate entries - Expected behavior for comparison functions

For studies needing SuSiE-based fine-mapping:



EUR ancestry recommended for predominantly European GWAS
ALL ancestry suffers from LD heterogeneity → weak/scattered credible sets
EUR shows strong colocalization (PP.H4 ~0.97) where ALL shows none (~0.001)





Filter variants by “Direction” column (from meta-analysis)
Keep variants present in most studies (≤2 “?” allowed)
Dramatically improves LD matrix eigenvalues





Critical: use fixref=TRUE in LD matrix creation
Aligns effect allele from sumstats to LD reference panel
Without this, SuSiE fails to converge

Prefer simple solutions over complex refactoring (KISS principle)




No Claude footer required. Keep messages concise and descriptive.

title: CLAUDE.md - genepicoloc Package Development
text:
genepicoloc is an R package for genetic colocalization analysis across multiple QTL and PheWAS datasets. It wraps the coloc package with scalable infrastructure for large-scale analyses.
Repository: https://github.com/genepi-freiburg/genepicoloc

title: CLAUDE.md - genepicoloc Package Development
text:
The package powers a large-scale colocalization analysis for the CKDGen Consortium (kidney function GWAS). Key achievements:



7 CKDGen traits: eGFRcr, eGFRcys, UACR, Calcium, Phosphate, Albumin, Urate
9 external data sources: GTEx eQTL, eQTLGen, UKB_PPP (proteomics), Icelanders pGWAS, FinnGen, MVP, UKB_TOPMed, GCKD metabolomics, CKDGen internal
~200,000+ colocalization tests per study
Results stored efficiently in tar archives with job-based parallelization




A production-ready Shiny app was developed: ckdgen-coloc-app/
Features: - Network visualization (region-centered and trait-centered views) - Interactive filtering by PP.H4 threshold and study - Regional Association Plots (LocusZoom-style) with interactive plotly - Gene Info Panel with annotations from NCBI, Reactome, HPO, HGNC - AI-powered insights (Claude API integration) - Docker deployment ready
Key files to reference: - /home/oleg/Work/bioinfo/ckdgen/git/followup/genepicoloc/ckdgen-coloc-app/ - Full Shiny app - /home/oleg/Work/bioinfo/ckdgen/git/followup/genepicoloc/ckdgen_genepicoloc_functions.R - CKDGen-specific helper functions



Successfully implemented regional plot data extraction:
Workflow: 1. Run genepicoloc with save_sumstats=TRUE to save sumstats_2 RDS files in tar archives 2. Extract regional data using optimized job-grouped extraction (5-10x faster) 3. Serve in Shiny app with interactive LocusZoom-style plots
Key optimizations discovered: - Group regions by job, extract tar once per job (not per region) - Deduplicate trait names before processing - Use filename for Icelanders_pGWAS (short name annotation has errors)

title: CLAUDE.md - genepicoloc Package Development
text:
Second use case: colocalization for GCKD multi-biomarker GWAS (VAE-derived modules).



ME19 and ME30 modules, SNPTEST format (.gwas), hg19, N=4,850
Severe p-value underflow: lead SNPs have p ~ 1e-1762 (z ~ 90)
pvalue_precise_normal column preserves full precision as text





Load .gwas file with fread()
Handle underflow: handle_underflow(pvalue_precise_normal, return_nlog10P = TRUE)
LiftOver hg19 -> hg38: genepi_liftover() + setDT() on result
Column mapping: SNP->rsID, coded_all->A1, noncoded_all->A2, AF_coded_all->AF, n_total->N
Build Name: chr:pos:ref:alt format





ME19: 1 region (chr2/NAT8), 7 high-confidence colocalizations (creatinine, eGFR, Mg, CKD, BUN, AST)
ME30: 2 regions (chr10/HPSE2 lead signal nlog10P=3274.8, chr2/NAT8 secondary), 12 high-confidence colocalizations
All colocalizations at chr2/NAT8 locus - kidney function signal
chr10/HPSE2 region: no MVP colocalizations (may colocalize with eQTL/pQTL)

Decision: Use SLURM array jobs for multi-node parallelization (not Nextflow/Snakemake)
Rationale: Workload is embarrassingly parallel (no inter-job dependencies), SLURM is already available, zero new dependencies
Approach (Option B - cleaner separation):

genepicoloc_prepare() - new function to save inputs and return job count
sbatch --array=1-N - SLURM submits jobs in parallel
genepicoloc_job_standalone(dir_out, job_idx) - each array task runs one job
consolidate_coloc_results() - unchanged, runs after all jobs complete

Status: Not yet implemented, planned as next major improvement





Batch tabix queries (1 call for all regions per file instead of per-region) - ~2-5x speedup
Pre-filter empty datasets before main run - variable speedup

title: CLAUDE.md - genepicoloc Package Development
text:
Converts genomic coordinates from hg19 to hg38 using UCSC liftOver.
Location: R/name_by_position.R
Usage:
sumstats_hg38 <- genepi_liftover(
  sumstats = sumstats,
  CHR_name = "Chr",
  POS_name = "Pos_b37",
  A1_name = "Allele1",
  A2_name = "Allele2",
  liftOver_bin = "/path/to/liftOver",
  liftOver_chain = "/path/to/hg19ToHg38.over.chain.gz"
)
Output columns: CHR_hg38, POS_hg38, A1_hg38, A2_hg38
Features: - Works on a copy (doesn’t modify input) - Proper temp file handling with on.exit() cleanup - Auto-detects chr prefix format - Handles strand flipping during liftOver
Required tools: - UCSC liftOver binary: https://hgdownload.soe.ucsc.edu/admin/exe/linux.x86_64/liftOver - Chain file: https://hgdownload.soe.ucsc.edu/goldenPath/hg19/liftOver/hg19ToHg38.over.chain.gz



Adds rsID and standardized variant names by querying Ensembl VCF files.
Location: R/name_by_position.R
Usage:
# Option 1: Single VCF file (e.g., chr21 only for testing)
sumstats_named <- name_by_position(
  sumstats = sumstats_hg38,
  CHR_name = "CHR_hg38",
  POS_name = "POS_hg38",
  A1_name = "A1_hg38",
  A2_name = "A2_hg38",
  dbSNP_file = "/path/to/homo_sapiens-chr21.vcf.gz"
)

# Option 2: Per-chromosome VCF directory (recommended for production)
sumstats_named <- name_by_position(
  sumstats = sumstats_hg38,
  CHR_name = "CHR_hg38",
  POS_name = "POS_hg38",
  A1_name = "A1_hg38",
  A2_name = "A2_hg38",
  dbSNP_dir = "/path/to/ensembl_vcf/"  # contains homo_sapiens-chr{1..22,X,Y,MT}.vcf.gz
)
Parameters: - dbSNP_file: Path to single tabix-indexed VCF file - dbSNP_dir: Path to directory with per-chromosome files (homo_sapiens-chr{CHR}.vcf.gz)
Output columns: rs (rsID), Name_hg38 (chr:pos:ref:alt format)
Features: - Works directly with Ensembl VCF files (no preprocessing needed) - Supports both single-file and per-chromosome directory modes - Auto-detects chromosome naming convention (chr21 vs 21) per file - Handles multi-allelic variants (splits comma-separated ALT alleles) - Matches alleles accounting for strand flipping - Skips chromosomes without corresponding VCF file (warns user) - No external dependencies (uses system2 + tabix)
Ensembl VCF source:
# For testing (chr21 only, ~165MB):
wget https://ftp.ensembl.org/pub/release-115/variation/vcf/homo_sapiens/homo_sapiens-chr21.vcf.gz
wget https://ftp.ensembl.org/pub/release-115/variation/vcf/homo_sapiens/homo_sapiens-chr21.vcf.gz.csi

# For production (all chromosomes, ~15GB total):
for chr in {1..22} X Y MT; do
  wget https://ftp.ensembl.org/pub/release-115/variation/vcf/homo_sapiens/homo_sapiens-chr${chr}.vcf.gz
  wget https://ftp.ensembl.org/pub/release-115/variation/vcf/homo_sapiens/homo_sapiens-chr${chr}.vcf.gz.csi
done
Performance (chr21): - Input: 112,722 variants - Matched: 112,722 variants (100%) - Processing time: ~2 minutes



library(data.table)
library(genepicoloc)

# 1. Load hg19 summary statistics
sumstats <- fread("gwas_hg19.txt.gz")

# 2. LiftOver to hg38
sumstats_hg38 <- genepi_liftover(
  sumstats = sumstats,
  CHR_name = "Chr",
  POS_name = "Pos_b37",
  A1_name = "Allele1",
  A2_name = "Allele2",
  liftOver_bin = "liftOver",
  liftOver_chain = "hg19ToHg38.over.chain.gz"
)

# 3. Add rsID and Name (optional, for display)
sumstats_named <- name_by_position(
  sumstats = sumstats_hg38,
  CHR_name = "CHR_hg38",
  POS_name = "POS_hg38",
  A1_name = "A1_hg38",
  A2_name = "A2_hg38",
  dbSNP_file = "homo_sapiens-chr21.vcf.gz"
)

# 4. Calculate -log10(P) with underflow handling
sumstats_named[, nlog10P := -log10(P)]
sumstats_named[!is.finite(nlog10P), nlog10P := max(nlog10P[is.finite(nlog10P)]) + 1]

title: CLAUDE.md - genepicoloc Package Development
text:
Results are stored in tar archives per job: - jobs/job_XXX/coloc_results.tar - Colocalization results - jobs/job_XXX/sumstats.tar - Sumstats for regional plots (if save_sumstats=TRUE) - jobs/job_XXX/sumstats_1.RDS - Source study sumstats



After colocalization, coloc_out_annotate() in genepicoloc_internal: 1. Reads consolidated results from dir_out/unfilt/ 2. Dispatches to study-specific annotator: - annotate_eQTL() - GTEx, eQTLGen, Kidney_eQTL (uses gencode_v26 for gene mapping) - annotate_pQTL() - UKB_PPP, Icelanders, GCKD_pGWAS (uses olink protein map) - annotation_std() - GWAS/PheWAS studies (uses annotation DB for phenotype names) 3. Writes annot/study_annot_unfilt.RDS 4. Filters in 3 tiers: coloc_done -> PP.H4 > 0.8 -> sumstats_2 genome-wide sig 5. Final: annot/annot_filt.RDS (also .xlsx)



genepi_liftover() returns data.frame - Returns base R data.frame, not data.table - Must call setDT() on result before using data.table syntax (:=, bare column names in [)
MVP annotation DB has old /data/ paths - annotation_db.RDS was created with old /data/ mount paths - Fix in arguments_custom.R: sub("^/data/", "/g/epi/data/", ...) on file paths from DB - Proper fix: regenerate annotation DB with create_annotation_db()
MVP ancestry-split annotation - MVP_R4_AFR/AMR/EAS/EUR/META studies share the MVP_R4 annotation DB entry - annotation_std() maps grepl("^MVP_R4_", study) to “MVP_R4” for DB lookup
P-value underflow handling - Some GWAS have p-values far beyond R’s numeric range (e.g., 1e-1762 for GCKD VAE) - handle_underflow() parses scientific notation text strings directly - Fallback from z-scores: nlog10P = -log10(2) - pnorm(abs(z), lower.tail=FALSE, log.p=TRUE) / log(10) - Always check for text-format p-value columns (e.g., pvalue_precise_normal in SNPTEST output)
MVP variant naming bug (historical) - Old MVP data had mismatched variant names - Solution: Always verify nsnps is reasonable (~6000, not ~20)
Non-positive definite LD matrices (coloc.susie) - Reference panel mismatch causes negative eigenvalues - Solution: Eigenvalue shift method in shift_eigenvalues_minimal()
Icelanders_pGWAS protein naming - Short name annotation has errors (wrong proteins labeled) - Solution: Use filename (contains SeqId + protein name)
UKB_PPP protein complex duplicates - Multi-protein assays (e.g., FUT3_FUT5) create duplicate entries - Expected behavior for comparison functions

title: CLAUDE.md - genepicoloc Package Development
text:
For studies needing SuSiE-based fine-mapping:



EUR ancestry recommended for predominantly European GWAS
ALL ancestry suffers from LD heterogeneity → weak/scattered credible sets
EUR shows strong colocalization (PP.H4 ~0.97) where ALL shows none (~0.001)





Filter variants by “Direction” column (from meta-analysis)
Keep variants present in most studies (≤2 “?” allowed)
Dramatically improves LD matrix eigenvalues





Critical: use fixref=TRUE in LD matrix creation
Aligns effect allele from sumstats to LD reference panel
Without this, SuSiE fails to converge
